title: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

seo.title: Classic Hummus Recipe: Easy & Authentic Middle Eastern Dip
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Classic Hummus Recipe at home! This easy, authentic Middle Eastern dip with tahini and olive oil is perfect for any occasion.
longTailKeywords: homemade hummus with tahini and olive oil
seo.h1: Classic Hummus Recipe: Authentic Middle Eastern Dip
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Creamiest Hummus
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic hummus recipe garnished with olive oil, paprika, and parsley, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this incredibly smooth and flavorful Classic Hummus Recipe, a staple Middle Eastern appetizer.

description: This Classic Hummus recipe brings the authentic taste of the Middle East right to your kitchen. Creamy, rich, and utterly delicious, it's perfect as a dip or spread for any occasion. Made with simple ingredients like chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and garlic, then finished with a generous drizzle of golden olive oil.

ingredients.notes: from 1 (15-ounce) can, drained and rinsed, skins removed for extra smooth hummus
peeled and roughly chopped, or to taste

instructions.title: Prepare the Chickpeas
instructions.text: If using canned chickpeas, drain and rinse them thoroughly. For the creamiest hummus, gently rub the chickpeas between your hands or with a towel to remove as many skins as possible. This step is crucial for a silky smooth texture.

instructions.title: Combine Tahini and Lemon
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the tahini, fresh lemon juice, chopped garlic, and salt. Process for about 1-2 minutes until the mixture is pale and thick, almost like whipped cream. This emulsifies the tahini and lemon, creating a light base.

instructions.title: Add Chickpeas and Cumin
instructions.text: Add the prepared chickpeas and ground cumin (if using) to the food processor. Process for 3-5 minutes, scraping down the sides as needed, until the mixture is very smooth. It will be quite thick at this stage.

instructions.title: Achieve Creamy Consistency
instructions.text: With the food processor running, slowly drizzle in 2-4 tablespoons of ice water, one tablespoon at a time, until the hummus reaches your desired creamy, airy consistency. The ice water helps to lighten the texture and whiten the color.

instructions.title: Taste and Adjust Seasoning
instructions.text: Stop the food processor and taste the hummus. Adjust with more salt, lemon juice, or garlic if necessary. Process for another 30 seconds to fully incorporate any adjustments. The flavor should be balanced and bright.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Transfer the hummus to a serving bowl. Use the back of a spoon to create a swirl or well in the center. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil, sprinkle with paprika, and garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately with warm pita bread or fresh vegetables.

tips: For the absolute smoothest hummus, meticulously remove the skins from the chickpeas after cooking or draining. This extra step makes all the difference in achieving a restaurant-quality texture.
Always use good quality tahini; its flavor is central to authentic hummus. Freshly squeezed lemon juice is also crucial for brightness and should not be substituted with bottled juice.

Hummus tastes best at room temperature. Let it sit out for 15-20 minutes before serving. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days, drizzling with a little olive oil before storing to prevent drying.
article: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil is a beloved Middle Eastern Appetizers dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
